message:
feat(doctor): implement agent auth functional tests

Add thorough authentication checks for AI coding agents:

check_claude_auth():
- Verifies binary works
- Checks ~/.claude/config.json exists
- Runs claude --print-system-info as low-cost validation

check_codex_auth():
- Verifies binary works
- Checks OPENAI_API_KEY environment variable
- Searches ~/.zshrc.local, ~/.config/openai/api_key

check_gemini_auth():
- Verifies binary works
- Checks GOOGLE_API_KEY or GEMINI_API_KEY
- Searches ~/.zshrc.local, gcloud credentials

Closes: agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-325

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-2bn.1","title":"Create onboard TUI shell script","description":"# Task: Create Onboard TUI Shell Script

## Description
Build the interactive onboarding TUI that teaches users the ACFS workflow.

## Location
Install to: `~/.local/bin/onboard`

## UX Flow

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│           Welcome to ACFS Onboarding                │
│                                                     │
│  Choose a lesson:                                   │
│                                                     │
│  [1] Welcome & Overview                ✓ completed  │
│  [2] Linux Navigation (5 min)          ● in progress│
│  [3] SSH & Persistence                              │
│  [4] tmux Basics                                    │
│  [5] Agent Commands (cc, cod, gmi)                  │
│  [6] NTM Command Center                             │
│  [7] NTM Prompt Palette                             │
│  [8] The Flywheel Loop                              │
│                                                     │
│  [q] Quit  [r] Restart from beginning               │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Implementation Options

### Option A: Pure Bash + gum
Use charmbracelet/gum for TUI elements:
```bash
gum choose "Welcome" "Linux Navigation" "SSH & Persistence" ...
```

### Option B: Simple menu
Plain bash with read:
```bash
while true; do
    clear
    echo "ACFS Onboarding"
    echo "1) Welcome"
    echo "2) Linux Navigation"
    ...
    read -p "Choose: " choice
done
```

## Lesson Format
Each lesson:
1. Brief explanation
2. Command to try
3. Expected output
4. Checkpoint/quiz

## Progress Tracking
Store in ~/.acfs/onboard_progress.json:
```json
{
  "completed": [1, 2, 3],
  "current": 4,
  "started_at": "2025-12-19T..."
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 8 lessons implemented
- [ ] Progress saved between runs
- [ ] Can be re-run anytime
- [ ] Works over SSH
- [ ] Clear and beginner-friendly","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:26:56.967996-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T12:17:32.895359-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T12:17:32.895359-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented interactive onboard TUI with: gum-based menu when available, fallback simple menu, progress tracking in ~/.acfs/onboard_progress.json, lesson completion markers, --reset flag, updated 1-8 numbering. Commit d96659e.","labels":["onboard","task"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-2bn.1","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-2bn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:26:56.969656-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-2du","title":"Set up bun workspaces for monorepo","description":"# Task: Set up Bun Workspaces

## Description
Configure bun workspaces in the root package.json to manage:
- apps/web (Next.js wizard website)
- packages/manifest (manifest utilities)
- packages/onboard (onboard TUI)

## Technical Details
Root package.json should include:
```json
{
  "workspaces": ["apps/*", "packages/*"]
}
```

Each workspace needs its own package.json with appropriate name/scripts.

## Reasoning
Bun workspaces allow:
- Shared dependencies across packages
- Single lockfile (bun.lock)
- Easy cross-package imports
- Consistent tooling

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Root package.json with workspaces config
- [ ] Each workspace has valid package.json
- [ ] `bun install` works from root
- [ ] Cross-package imports resolve correctly

## Dependencies
Depends on directory structure being created first.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:20:33.61383-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T23:27:33.126119-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T23:27:33.126119-05:00","close_reason":"Root bun workspace set up with apps/* and packages/* workspaces","labels":["infrastructure","task"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-2du","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-e88","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:20:33.616805-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-31e","title":"Installer: implement (or de-scope) cloud/db installs + skip flags","description":"install.sh defines --skip-postgres/--skip-vault/--skip-cloud and smoke test references them, but install.sh never installs PostgreSQL/Vault/cloud CLIs. scripts/lib/cloud_db.sh + acfs.manifest.yaml define these installs, and README claims they're installed.","acceptance_criteria":"Either:
- installer runs cloud/db phase (respecting skip flags) without breaking Ubuntu 24.04/25.x CI, OR
- docs/manifest updated to match reality and flags removed/hidden.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-20T14:17:29.066623-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T14:37:42.698153-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T14:37:42.698153-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented cloud/db phase in install.sh (Postgres 18 via PGDG, Vault via HashiCorp repo, cloud CLIs via bun) honoring skip flags; updated smoke test + phase numbering."}
-{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-325","title":"Implement agent auth functional tests","description":"# Task: Implement agent auth functional tests

## Context
Part of EPIC: Enhanced Doctor with Functional Tests (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-t9i)

## What to Do
Create functional tests for AI agent authentication:

### check_claude_auth()
1. Check if claude binary exists (skip if not)
2. Check if ~/.claude/config.json exists
3. Try claude --print-system-info (low-cost API check)
4. Return: 0=OK, 1=fail, 2=skip

### check_codex_auth()
1. Check if codex binary exists
2. Check for OPENAI_API_KEY env var
3. Check for API key in ~/.zshrc.local
4. Return status

### check_gemini_auth()
1. Check if gemini binary exists
2. Check for GOOGLE_API_KEY or GEMINI_API_KEY
3. Check for credentials file
4. Return status

## Acceptance Criteria
- Each check has clear pass/fail criteria
- Skipped if tool not installed (status 2)
- Auth issues detected reliably
- No false positives

## Files to Modify
- scripts/lib/doctor.sh: Add check functions","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:45:18.598224-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T12:45:18.598224-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-325","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-01s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:47:34.958137-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

Make --json emit only valid JSON on stdout with correct summary counts (and handle missing /etc/os-release).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"FuchsiaCreek","created_at":"2025-12-20T13:44:19.646755-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T13:49:30.854595-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T13:49:30.854595-05:00","close_reason":"Fixed scripts/lib/doctor.sh --json to emit pure valid JSON (no headings), increment counters in JSON mode, and JSON-escape strings; also handles missing /etc/os-release."}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-3ht","title":"Implement cloud CLI updates (vercel, wrangler, supabase, fly)","description":"## What
Implement the 'cloud' category for acfs-update:
- Vercel CLI: bun update -g vercel
- Wrangler (Cloudflare): bun update -g wrangler
- Supabase CLI: bun update -g supabase
- Fly CLI: Reinstall via official installer (flyctl self-upgrade)
- AWS CLI: Detect install method, update accordingly

## AWS CLI Handling (added)
AWS CLI can be installed multiple ways:
1. apt-installed: Handled by system updates (apt upgrade)
2. pip-installed: pip install --upgrade awscli
3. Official installer: Re-run installer or use aws --update

Detection: Check `which aws`, then `dpkg -s awscli` vs `pip show awscli`

## Technical Details
- Most cloud CLIs are bun global packages - simple updates
- Fly CLI has built-in: flyctl self-upgrade (or reinstall)
- Cloud CLIs may have authentication state - preserve it

## Considerations
- Only update tools that are installed
- Skip tools without prompting if not present
- For bun packages: bun update -g <pkg>
- Log version changes

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All cloud CLIs update correctly
- [ ] AWS CLI detected and updated properly
- [ ] Skips uninstalled CLIs
- [ ] Authentication preserved
- [ ] Version changes logged","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:26:43.995628-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T13:32:54.644818-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-3ht","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-75e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:27:25.514415-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-3mp","title":"Implement runtime updates (bun, rust, uv, go)","description":"## What
Implement the 'runtimes' category for acfs-update:
- Bun: bun upgrade
- Rust: rustup update (stable, nightly if installed)
- uv: uv self update (or reinstall via official installer)
- Go: apt handles if system-installed, skip otherwise

## Technical Details
- Each runtime has different update mechanisms
- Bun: ~/.bun/bin/bun upgrade
- Rust: ~/.cargo/bin/rustup update
- uv: May need to source the installer again
- Go: Typically managed via apt, but could be manual install

## Considerations
- Must run as target user, not root (except go via apt)
- Capture before/after versions for each
- Skip if tool not installed (detected by missing binary)
- Rust may update multiple toolchains - capture all

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Each runtime updates correctly
- [ ] Skips uninstalled runtimes gracefully
- [ ] Version changes logged
- [ ] Works for both root and non-root users","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:26:07.808375-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T13:26:07.808375-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-3mp","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-75e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:27:24.85834-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-azw","title":"Implement database and cloud CLI functional tests","description":"# Task: Implement database and cloud CLI functional tests

## Context
Part of EPIC: Enhanced Doctor with Functional Tests (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-t9i)

## What to Do
Create functional tests for databases and cloud CLIs:

### PostgreSQL Tests
- check_postgres_connection(): psql -c "SELECT 1"
- check_postgres_role(): Verify ubuntu role exists

### Cloud CLI Tests
- check_vault_configured(): VAULT_ADDR set (info status if not)
- check_wrangler_auth(): wrangler whoami
- check_supabase_auth(): Access token exists and not expired
- check_vercel_auth(): vercel whoami

### Status Codes
- 0: Pass
- 1: Fail (with fix suggestion)
- 2: Skip (not installed)
- 3: Info (not configured, but optional)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Each cloud CLI has auth check
- PostgreSQL connection and role verified
- Status codes used consistently
- Timeout on external calls (prevent hangs)

 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-c61","title":"Define session export JSON schema","description":"# Task: Define session export JSON schema

## Context
Part of EPIC: Agent Session Sharing and Replay (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-0sb)

## What to Do
Design the JSON schema for exported agent sessions.

## Schema (inline in export code)

```typescript
// In scripts/lib/session.sh or equivalent
// Schema documented here, not as separate file

interface SessionExport {
    schema_version: 1;
    exported_at: string;  // ISO8601
    session_id: string;
    agent: "claude-code" | "codex" | "gemini";
    model: string;
    summary: string;
    duration_minutes: number;
    stats: {
        turns: number;
        files_created: number;
        files_modified: number;
        commands_run: number;
    };
    outcomes: Array<{
        type: "file_created" | "file_modified" | "command_run";
        path?: string;
        description: string;
    }>;
    key_prompts: string[];
    sanitized_transcript: Array<{
        role: "user" | "assistant";
        content: string;
        timestamp: string;
    }>;
}
```

## Key Design Decisions

1. **Schema lives in code, not separate file**
   - AGENTS.md: "bar for adding files is very high"
   - Schema is simple enough to document inline
   - TypeScript interface provides validation

2. **Version field for evolution**
   - `schema_version: 1` allows future changes
   - Importers can handle version differences

3. **Sanitization-aware**
   - Fields designed for post-sanitization data
   - No raw command outputs (may contain secrets)

4. **Focused on value**
   - `outcomes` shows what happened
   - `key_prompts` shows how (for learning)
   - Not a raw dump of everything

## Validation
```bash
validate_session_export() {
    local file="$1"
    
    # Check required fields exist
    jq -e ".schema_version and .session_id and .agent" "$file" >/dev/null || {
        log_error "Invalid session export: missing required fields"
        return 1
    }
    
    # Check version
    local version=$(jq -r ".schema_version" "$file")
    [[ "$version" == "1" ]] || {
        log_warn "Session version $version may not be fully compatible"
    }
    
    return 0
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Schema defined as TypeScript interface (in code comments)
- [ ] Validation function checks required fields
- [ ] Version field present for future evolution
- [ ] NO separate schema.json file
- [ ] NO separate docs file (documented in bead + code)

## Files to Modify (NOT create)
- scripts/lib/session.sh - add validation and schema comments","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:46:36.450112-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T13:02:33.06774-05:00"}
-{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-coj","title":"Create GitHub Action for daily checksum monitoring","description":"# Task: Create GitHub Action for daily checksum monitoring

## Purpose
Automatically detect when upstream installer scripts change and create PRs to update checksums.

## Critical Addition: Verify Stability Before PR

**Problem:** Upstream scripts might change multiple times in a day during a release.
**Solution:** Verify checksum is stable across 2 checks before creating PR.

## Workflow Design

```yaml
name: Monitor Upstream Checksums
on:
  schedule:
    - cron: "0 6 * * *"   # 6 AM UTC daily
    - cron: "0 18 * * *"  # 6 PM UTC daily (stability check)
  workflow_dispatch:       # Manual trigger

jobs:
  check-checksums:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Fetch current checksums
        id: current
        run: |
          ./scripts/lib/security.sh --verify --json > current.json
          
      - name: Check for changes
        id: changes
        run: |
          # Parse JSON output
          changed=$(jq -r ".changed | length" current.json)
          echo "changed_count=$changed" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          
          if [[ "$changed" -gt 0 ]]; then
            echo "changed=true" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
            jq -r ".changed[].name" current.json > changed_tools.txt
          else
            echo "changed=false" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          fi
          
      - name: Check stability (only if changes detected)
        if: steps.changes.outputs.changed == true
        id: stability
        run: |
          # Load previous run checksums from cache
          if [[ -f ".checksum-cache/previous.json" ]]; then
            prev_checksums=$(jq -r ".checksums" .checksum-cache/previous.json)
            curr_checksums=$(jq -r ".checksums" current.json)
            
            if [[ "$prev_checksums" == "$curr_checksums" ]]; then
              echo "stable=true" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
              echo "Checksums stable across 2 runs - safe to PR"
            else
              echo "stable=false" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
              echo "Checksums changed since last run - waiting for stability"
            fi
          else
            echo "stable=false" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
            echo "First run with changes - caching for stability check"
          fi
          
          # Cache current state
          mkdir -p .checksum-cache
          cp current.json .checksum-cache/previous.json
          
      - name: Update checksums file
        if: steps.changes.outputs.changed == true && steps.stability.outputs.stable == true
        run: |
          ./scripts/lib/security.sh --update-checksums > checksums.yaml
          
      - name: Create Pull Request
        if: steps.changes.outputs.changed == true && steps.stability.outputs.stable == true
        uses: peter-evans/create-pull-request@v5
        with:
          title: "chore(security): update upstream checksums"
          body: |
            ## Upstream Checksum Updates
            
            The following installer checksums have changed:
            $(cat changed_tools.txt | sed "s/^/- /")
            
            ### Stability Verified
            ✅ Checksums stable across 2 consecutive checks (12 hours apart)
            
            ### Action Required
            Please review the upstream changes before merging:
            $(cat changed_tools.txt | while read tool; do
              url=$(jq -r ".installers.$tool.url" checksums.yaml)
              echo "- [$tool]($url)"
            done)
            
            ---
            🤖 Auto-generated by checksum monitoring workflow
          branch: auto/update-checksums
          labels: security, automated
```

## Stability Check Logic

```
Run 1 (6 AM): Detects bun checksum changed
  → Cache new checksum
  → No PR yet

Run 2 (6 PM): Same bun checksum as cached
  → Stable = true
  → Create PR

Run 2 (6 PM): Different bun checksum than cached  
  → Stable = false
  → Update cache
  → Wait for next run
```

## Dependencies
- Depends on: JSON output mode (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-ss3)
- Creates: Auto-generated PRs

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Runs twice daily (12 hours apart)
- [ ] Stability check requires 2 matching runs
- [ ] Only creates PR when checksums are stable
- [ ] PR body lists changed tools with links
- [ ] Manual trigger available for testing","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:45:53.040595-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T14:51:36.491452-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-coj","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-ss3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:47:37.794807-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

```yaml
          ./scripts/lib/security.sh --update-checksums > checksums.yaml
          
      - name: Create Pull Request
        if: steps.changes.outputs.changed == true && steps.stability.outputs.stable == true
        uses: peter-evans/create-pull-request@v5
        with:
          title: "chore(security): update upstream checksums"
          body: |

```

## Stability Check Logic

```
Run 1 (6 AM): Detects bun checksum changed
  → Cache new checksum
  → No PR yet

Run 2 (6 PM): Same bun checksum as cached
  → Stable = true
  → Create PR

Run 2 (6 PM): Different bun checksum than cached  
  → Stable = false
  → Update cache
  → Wait for next run
```

## Dependencies
- Depends on: JSON output mode (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-ss3)
- Creates: Auto-generated PRs

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Runs twice daily (12 hours apart)
- [ ] Stability check requires 2 matching runs
- [ ] Only creates PR when checksums are stable
- [ ] PR body lists changed tools with links
- [ ] Manual trigger available for testing","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:45:53.040595-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T14:55:17.637256-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T14:55:17.637256-05:00","close_reason":"GitHub Action workflow created for daily checksum monitoring with stability checking","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-coj","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-ss3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:47:37.794807-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-csv","title":"Wire acfs-update into install.sh and PATH","description":"## What
Integrate acfs-update into the ACFS installation process:
1. Copy acfs-update script to ~/.acfs/bin/
2. Ensure ~/.acfs/bin is in PATH (already done via acfs.zshrc)
3. Create 'acfs' wrapper script that supports subcommands (acfs update, acfs doctor, etc.)

## Technical Details
- Script lives at ~/.acfs/bin/acfs-update
- May want 'acfs' wrapper: acfs update -> acfs-update, acfs doctor -> acfs-doctor
- install.sh copies script during finalize phase
- Script should be executable (chmod +x)

## Considerations
- Should we have acfs-update standalone or as 'acfs update' subcommand?
- Subcommand approach is more elegant but requires wrapper script
- For v1, standalone acfs-update is simpler

## Decision
v1: Standalone acfs-update script
v2: Add 'acfs' wrapper with subcommands

- [ ] Executable and in PATH after install


```bash
    # Check required fields exist

}
```

- Version mismatch detected and handled
- User prompted before discarding state
- Clear log message for debugging

 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-d0g","title":"Implement Dicklesworthstone stack updates (checksum-aware)","description":"## What
Implement the 'stack' category for acfs-update with SECURE checksum verification.

Tools to update:
- NTM (Named Tmux Manager)
- MCP Agent Mail
- UBS (Ultimate Bug Scanner)
- BV (Beads Viewer)
- CASS (Coding Agent Session Search)
- CM (CASS Memory System)
- CAAM (Coding Agent Account Manager)
- SLB (Simultaneous Launch Button)
- BD (Beads CLI) - the task tracker itself

## SECURITY: Checksum-Aware Update Flow
Unlike the "bypass checksums" approach, we MUST verify checksums to prevent executing compromised scripts:

1. Check if tool is installed (binary exists)
2. If not installed: skip (acfs-update doesn't install new tools)
3. If installed:
   a. Fetch installer script from GitHub
   b. Calculate SHA256 checksum
   c. Compare to checksums.yaml:
      - MATCH: Run installer safely (known-good script)
      - MISMATCH: Script changed upstream. Options:
        * Show warning with URL to review changes
        * If --yes: proceed (user accepts risk)
        * If interactive: prompt [S]kip / [P]roceed / [V]iew diff
        * Default: Skip and continue with other tools

## Why This Matters
Bypassing checksums would allow an attacker who compromises a GitHub repo to execute arbitrary code on all users who run `acfs-update`. The tradeoff of prompting on upstream changes is worth the security.

## Special Cases
- MCP Agent Mail: git pull + uv sync in ~/mcp_agent_mail (no installer re-run)
- BD (Beads): Check where installed, use appropriate update method

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All 9 stack tools update correctly
- [ ] Checksum verification works
- [ ] User prompted on checksum mismatch (unless --yes)
- [ ] MCP Agent Mail git+uv flow works
- [ ] Skips uninstalled tools
- [ ] Version changes logged","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:32:07.801083-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T13:32:07.801083-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-d0g","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-75e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:32:19.582864-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

-{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-nna","title":"Add automatic retry for transient network errors","description":"# Task: Add automatic retry for transient network errors

## Purpose
Handle temporary network issues gracefully without user intervention.

## Retry Strategy

### Timing: Immediate → 5s → 15s
**Rationale for these intervals:**
- **Immediate retry (0s):** Many transient errors clear instantly (TCP reset, brief DNS hiccup)
- **5 second wait:** Enough for most CDN/routing issues to resolve
- **15 second wait:** Handles rate limiting, brief outages
- **Total wait:** 20 seconds max - fast enough to not frustrate users

```bash
RETRY_DELAYS=(0 5 15)  # Immediate, then 5s, then 15s

retry_with_backoff() {
    local cmd="$1"
    local description="$2"
    local max_attempts=${#RETRY_DELAYS[@]}
    
    for ((attempt=0; attempt<max_attempts; attempt++)); do
        local delay=${RETRY_DELAYS[$attempt]}
        
        if ((attempt > 0)); then
            log_info "Retry $attempt/$((max_attempts-1)) for $description (waiting ${delay}s)..."
            sleep "$delay"
        fi
        
        if eval "$cmd" 2>/dev/null; then
            ((attempt > 0)) && log_info "Succeeded on retry $attempt"
            return 0
        fi
    done
    
    return 1
}
```

### Retryable Errors
Only retry for transient network issues:
```bash
is_retryable_error() {
    local exit_code="$1"
    local stderr="$2"
    
    # curl exit codes for network issues
    case "$exit_code" in
        6)  return 0 ;;  # Could not resolve host
        7)  return 0 ;;  # Failed to connect
        28) return 0 ;;  # Operation timeout
        35) return 0 ;;  # SSL connect error
        52) return 0 ;;  # Empty reply
        56) return 0 ;;  # Network receive error
    esac
    
    # Check stderr for common transient messages
    if [[ "$stderr" =~ (timeout|timed.out|connection.refused|temporarily) ]]; then
        return 0
    fi
    
    return 1  # Not retryable
}
```

### Integration with fetch_and_run()
```bash
verified_fetch_and_run() {
    local url="$1"
    local checksum="$2"
    local name="$3"
    shift 3
    
    retry_with_backoff \
        "verify_checksum \"$url\" \"$checksum\" \"$name\"" \
        "fetching $name"
    
    local status=$?
    if ((status \!= 0)); then
        log_error "Failed to fetch $name after ${#RETRY_DELAYS[@]} attempts"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Content already verified, now run
    verified_content | bash -s -- "$@"
}
```

## Non-Retryable Scenarios
- HTTP 404 (script doesnt exist)
- HTTP 403 (permission denied)
- Checksum mismatch (not a network issue)
- Script execution errors

## Dependencies
- Used by: security.sh fetch functions
- Used by: install.sh download operations

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Retry delays: 0s, 5s, 15s (total 20s max)
- [ ] Only retries network-related curl errors
- [ ] Clear logging of retry attempts
- [ ] Does not retry checksum mismatches
- [ ] Does not retry HTTP 4xx errors","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:50:37.817313-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T12:59:38.211364-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-nna","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-qqo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:51:28.919648-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-obi","title":"Implement Dicklesworthstone stack updates","description":"## What
Implement the 'stack' category for acfs-update. This is the most complex category because these tools use verified installers.

Tools to update:
- NTM (Named Tmux Manager)
- MCP Agent Mail
- UBS (Ultimate Bug Scanner)
- BV (Beads Viewer)
- CASS (Coding Agent Session Search)
- CM (CASS Memory System)
- CAAM (Coding Agent Account Manager)
- SLB (Simultaneous Launch Button)

## Technical Details
- Most tools: Re-run installer from GitHub (handles update logic internally)
- MCP Agent Mail: Special case - git pull + uv sync in ~/mcp_agent_mail
- For updates (not fresh installs), we trust the GitHub repos directly
- No checksum verification needed for updates - installer scripts are trusted

## Update Flow for Each Tool
1. Check if installed (binary exists)
2. Capture current version
3. Run installer with appropriate flags (e.g., --update, --yes)
4. Capture new version
5. Log the change

## Considerations
- Unlike fresh installs, we don't verify checksums for updates
- The installer scripts themselves handle idempotent updates
- MCP Agent Mail is different (git-based, not binary releases)
- Some tools may not have --update flag - reinstall is safe

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All 8 stack tools update correctly
- [ ] MCP Agent Mail git pull + uv sync works
- [ ] Skips uninstalled tools
- [ ] Version changes logged
- [ ] No checksum errors (we bypass for updates)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:26:42.374382-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T13:32:19.419493-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T13:32:19.419493-05:00","close_reason":"Replaced by agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-d0g which includes proper checksum verification. The original 'bypass checksums' approach was a security vulnerability.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-obi","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-75e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:27:25.347073-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-pom","title":"Implement ACFS self-update","description":"## What
Before updating other components, acfs-update should update itself:
1. git pull in ~/.acfs (or wherever ACFS is installed)
2. Re-source the update script if it changed
3. Continue with other updates using latest logic

## Why This Matters
- Ensures we have the latest update logic before updating tools
- Fixes any bugs in the update process itself
- Keeps checksums.yaml current (though not used for updates)

## Technical Details
- ACFS installed at ~/.acfs or detected via ACFS_HOME env var
- git pull --ff-only to avoid merge conflicts
- If update script changed, need to re-exec with same args

## Considerations
- What if git pull fails? (network, conflicts)
- Should we require this, or make it optional?
- Re-exec is tricky - need to preserve all args and state

## Decision
Make self-update automatic but non-fatal. If it fails, warn and continue with current version.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] ACFS self-updates on each run
- [ ] Handles git pull failures gracefully
- [ ] Re-exec works if script changed (or skip re-exec v1)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:26:45.271437-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T13:31:43.349328-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T13:31:43.349328-05:00","close_reason":"Out of scope for v1. ACFS is not git-cloned to ~/.acfs, so self-update requires re-running the installer. Users can do this manually. Consider for v2 after changing install to use git clone.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-pom","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-75e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:27:25.670496-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yad.9","title":"Implement wizard Steps 8-10: Reconnect, Status, Onboard","description":"# Task: Implement Wizard Steps 8-10

## Description
Complete the final wizard steps:
- Step 8: Reconnect as ubuntu
- Step 9: ACFS Status Check
- Step 10: Run onboard

## Step 8: Reconnect as Ubuntu
**Title:** Reconnect as ubuntu
**Body:**
If you installed while logged in as `root`, reconnect as `ubuntu`:
```bash
exit
ssh -i ~/.ssh/acfs_ed25519 ubuntu@YOUR_IP
```

**Skip if:** Already logged in as ubuntu

## Step 9: ACFS Status Check
**Title:** Verify your VPS is ready (one command)
**Body:**
Run this on your VPS:
```bash
acfs doctor
```

**What you should see:**
A checklist with lots of `PASS ✅` lines.

**Interactive checklist** - shows all installed tools:
- Identity & permissions
- Shell setup
- Core tools
- Coding agents
- Cloud/DB tools
- Dicklesworthstone stack

**Optional:** Paste JSON output for auto-check

## Step 10: Run Onboard + Start Vibe Coding
**Title:** You're ready! Start the tutorial
**Command:**
```bash
onboard
```

**Quick reference:**
- `cc` - Claude Code
- `cod` - Codex CLI
- `gmi` - Gemini CLI
- `ntm` - Named Tmux Manager
- `cass` - Session search
- `cm` - Memory system
- `ubs` - Bug scanner
- `bv` - Beads viewer

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Step 8: Skip option for non-root users
- [ ] Step 9: Full status checklist display
- [ ] Step 9: Optional JSON paste auto-check
- [ ] Step 10: Celebration UI (confetti?)
- [ ] Step 10: Quick reference card","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:24:04.4838-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T22:36:41.482071-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T22:36:41.482073-05:00","labels":["task","website","wizard-step"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yad.9","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yad","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:24:04.48597-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yaj","title":"Implement run_phase() wrapper and phase skipping","description":"# Task: Implement run_phase() wrapper and phase skipping

## Context
Part of EPIC: Phase-Granular Progress Persistence (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5uu)

## What to Do
Create run_phase() function that:
1. Checks if phase is already completed (is_phase_completed)
2. If completed: log 'Phase N already done, skipping' and return 0
3. If not completed: set CURRENT_PHASE, save state, run phase function
4. On success: add to COMPLETED_PHASES, save state
5. On failure: set FAILED_PHASE, save state, return 1

### Helper Functions
- is_phase_completed(phase_num): Check if phase in COMPLETED_PHASES
- complete_phase(phase_num): Add to completed, increment current, save

## Acceptance Criteria
- Completed phases are skipped on resume
- Current phase tracked before execution
- Phase completion persisted immediately (before next phase)
- Failure state recorded for debugging

## Usage Example
```bash
run_phase 1 "User Normalization" install_phase_1
run_phase 2 "APT Packages" install_phase_2
run_phase 3 "Shell Setup" install_phase_3
# ... etc
```

## Files to Modify
- install.sh: Wrap all phase calls with run_phase()","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:42:41.718027-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T14:42:02.906151-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T14:42:02.906151-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented run_phase() wrapper in scripts/lib/state.sh with phase skipping, state tracking, timing, and logging integration.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yaj","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-uxc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:47:27.913015-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yh5","title":"run_as_user: make curl|bash pipelines fail fast","description":"Several scripts/lib/* helpers run commands as TARGET_USER via `bash -c "$cmd"` (agents.sh, languages.sh, cli_tools.sh, cloud_db.sh, stack.sh). That new bash does not inherit pipefail, so `curl ... | bash` can return success when curl fails (bash runs empty input). Prefix the spawned shell with `set -o pipefail;` so pipeline failures propagate correctly.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"FuchsiaCreek","created_at":"2025-12-20T17:21:53.334333-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T17:35:11.544723-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T17:35:11.544723-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented on main in commit 0de3e7330b2d133789f85167c063c0c9bee7cd52 (adds set -o pipefail to run-as-user helpers)."}
-{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-ywk","title":"Create integration test for resume behavior","description":"# Task: Create integration test for resume behavior

## Context
Part of EPIC: Phase-Granular Progress Persistence (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5uu)

## Why Testing is Critical
Resume behavior is complex and bugs could cause:
- Users losing progress (defeats the purpose)
- Infinite loops (always resumes to same point)
- Skipping phases that weren't complete
- Data corruption in state file

## Test Scenarios

### 1. Normal resume
- Run install, stop after phase 5
- Re-run, verify phases 1-5 skipped, 6+ run

### 2. Corrupted state
- Create invalid state.json
- Run install, verify recovery prompt shown

### 3. Force reinstall
- Run install, stop after phase 5
- Re-run with --force-reinstall
- Verify all phases run

### 4. Interrupt during phase
- Run install, interrupt mid-phase
- Re-run, verify that phase runs again

### 5. Version mismatch
- Create state.json with different version
- Verify warning shown

## Implementation
Add to tests/vm/test_install_ubuntu.sh or create tests/resume_test.sh

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 5 scenarios tested
- Tests can run in CI (Docker)
- Clear pass/fail output
- Tests don't modify production state

## Files to Create
- tests/resume_test.sh or modify existing test script","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:51:54.987501-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T12:51:54.987501-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-ywk","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-yaj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:52:06.405711-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

+# check_claude_auth - Thorough Claude Code authentication check
+# Returns via check(): pass (auth OK), warn (partial/skipped), fail (auth broken)
+# Related: bead 325
+check_claude_auth() {
+    # Skip if not installed
+    if ! command -v claude &>/dev/null; then
+        check "deep.agent.claude_auth" "Claude Code" "warn" "not installed" "bun install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code"
+        return
+    fi
+
+    # Check if binary works
+    if ! claude --version &>/dev/null 2>&1; then
+        check "deep.agent.claude_auth" "Claude Code auth" "fail" "binary error" "Reinstall: bun install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code"
+        return
+    fi
+
+    # Check for config file (indicates previous auth)
+    local config_file="$HOME/.claude/config.json"
+    if [[ ! -f "$config_file" ]]; then
+        check "deep.agent.claude_auth" "Claude Code auth" "warn" "no config file" "Run: claude to authenticate"
+        return
+    fi
+
+    # Try low-cost API check: --print-system-info doesn't make API calls but verifies setup
+    if timeout 5 claude --print-system-info &>/dev/null 2>&1; then
+        check "deep.agent.claude_auth" "Claude Code auth" "pass" "authenticated"
     else
-        check "deep.agent.claude_auth" "Claude Code auth" "warn" "not installed"
+        # Config exists but system info fails - partial setup
+        check "deep.agent.claude_auth" "Claude Code auth" "warn" "config exists, verify failed" "Run: claude to re-authenticate"
     fi
+}

+# check_gemini_auth - Thorough Gemini CLI authentication check
+# Returns via check(): pass (auth OK), warn (partial/skipped), fail (auth broken)
+# Related: bead 325
+check_gemini_auth() {
+    # Skip if not installed
+    if ! command -v gemini &>/dev/null; then
+        check "deep.agent.gemini_auth" "Gemini CLI" "warn" "not installed" "bun install -g @google/gemini-cli@latest"
+        return
+    fi
+
+    # Check if binary works
+    if ! gemini --version &>/dev/null 2>&1; then
+        check "deep.agent.gemini_auth" "Gemini CLI auth" "fail" "binary error" "Reinstall: bun install -g @google/gemini-cli@latest"
+        return
+    fi
+
+    # Check for GOOGLE_API_KEY or GEMINI_API_KEY in environment
+    if [[ -n "${GOOGLE_API_KEY:-}" ]] || [[ -n "${GEMINI_API_KEY:-}" ]]; then
+        local key_name="GOOGLE_API_KEY"
+        [[ -n "${GEMINI_API_KEY:-}" ]] && key_name="GEMINI_API_KEY"
+        check "deep.agent.gemini_auth" "Gemini CLI auth" "pass" "$key_name set"
+        return
+    fi
+
+    # Check for API key in common config locations
+    local found_key=false
+
+    # Check ~/.zshrc.local (ACFS convention)
+    if [[ -f "$HOME/.zshrc.local" ]]; then
+        if grep -qE "(GOOGLE_API_KEY|GEMINI_API_KEY)" "$HOME/.zshrc.local" 2>/dev/null; then
+            found_key=true
         fi
+    fi
+
+    # Check Google Cloud application default credentials
+    if [[ -f "$HOME/.config/gcloud/application_default_credentials.json" ]]; then
+        found_key=true
+    fi
+
+    # Check for Gemini-specific config
+    if [[ -d "$HOME/.config/gemini" ]] || [[ -f "$HOME/.gemini/config" ]]; then
+        found_key=true
+    fi
+
+    if [[ "$found_key" == "true" ]]; then
+        check "deep.agent.gemini_auth" "Gemini CLI auth" "pass" "credentials found"
     else
-        check "deep.agent.gemini_auth" "Gemini CLI auth" "warn" "not installed"
+        check "deep.agent.gemini_auth" "Gemini CLI auth" "warn" "no API key found" "Set GOOGLE_API_KEY in ~/.zshrc.local"
     fi
 }
